CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, param_name VARCHAR(50), param_value INT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, param_name, param_value) VALUES (1, 'enable_qp', 16);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, param_name, param_value) VALUES (2, 'enable_logging', 1);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, param_name, param_value) VALUES (3, 'max_connections', 100);
UPDATE test_table SET param_value = 32 WHERE param_name = 'enable_qp';
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE param_value > (SELECT AVG(param_value) FROM test_table);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE param_name LIKE '%enable%';
SELECT param_name, param_value FROM test_table WHERE param_value IN (SELECT param_value FROM test_table WHERE param_name = 'enable_logging');
SELECT DISTINCT param_name FROM test_table WHERE param_value BETWEEN 1 AND 50;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, description VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO another_table (id, description) VALUES (1, 'Test Description 1');
INSERT INTO another_table (id, description) VALUES (2, 'Test Description 2');
SELECT a.id, a.description, b.param_value FROM another_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.id = b.id WHERE b.param_value >= 16;
UPDATE another_table SET description = 'Updated Description' WHERE id = (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE param_name = 'max_connections');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE param_name IN (SELECT DISTINCT param_name FROM test_table WHERE LEN(param_name) > 5) ORDER BY param_value DESC;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE enabled = TRUE AND param_value IS NOT NULL;
